reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about the increased size of struct zpdesc and the potential impact on performance. Suggested exploring alternative solutions to achieve the desired accounting functionality without incurring significant overhead.

2026-02-26.patch_summary: This patch series introduces per-memcg-lruvec zswap accounting by adding a new array of objcg pointers to the zpdesc structure, allowing for accurate tracking of memory usage by zswap and reducing NR_ZSWAP. The changes also move memcg charges to the zsmalloc layer, which is the only user of zswap at this time.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the performance differences observed in microbenchmarks are likely negligible and will be even smaller in production workloads due to the infrequent nature of objcgroup access.
sentiment_signals: performance differences are within a small fraction of the standard deviation
reviews.raw_body:
Sounds like any meagre performance difference is smaller than noise :P
If it's this negligible on these microbenchmarks, I think they'll be
infinitesimal in production workloads where these operations are a
very small part.

Kinda makes sense, because objcgroup access is only done in very small
subsets of operations: zswap entry store and zswap entry free, which
can only happen once each per zswap entry.

I think we're fine, but I'll leave other reviewers comment on it as well.

2026-03-03.patch_summary: This patch series introduces per-memcg-lruvec zswap accounting, which allows the Linux kernel to track and manage compressed memory usage at a more granular level. The goal is to improve memory management and reduce overhead in systems with multiple NUMA nodes. The patches include benchmark results that show minimal regressions (< 1%) and even some improvements in standard deviation for certain benchmarks. The approach taken involves modifying the zswap and zsmalloc code to account for per-memcg-lruvec usage, which should lead to better memory allocation and deallocation decisions.
